ocr.text: The analysis presented in the accompanying table highlights the median improvement in Catquest-9SF scores across various
cohorts affected by the H1N1 virus, specifically differentiating between individuals with and without comorbidities. Notably,
the data reveals that participants classified as asymptomatic nonshedders exhibited a median improvement of 1.92 in the
Catquest-9SF score when comorbidities were present, contrasting with a more substantial improvement of 3.57 in the absence
of such conditions. This disparity suggests a significant influence of comorbidities on the perceived quality of care indicators
and overall health outcomes following viral challenges.
First eye  Second eye
With comorbidity  Without comorbidity  With comorbidity  Without comorbidity
Median improvement in Catquest-9SF score  1.92  3.57  1.44  2.94
Cohort  Group  Virus  Tissue  Asymptomatic nonshedders  Symptomatic shedders  Platform  Citations
GSE73072 challenge A  Discovery challenge  H1N1  Whole blood  6  8  Affymetrix  [2, 3, 6]
GSE73072 challenge B  Discovery challenge  H3N2  Whole blood  6  7  Affymetrix  [2, 3, 6]
GSE73072 challenge C  Discovery challenge  H3N2  Whole blood  6  7  Affymetrix  [6]
GSE61754  Validation challenge  H3N2  Whole blood  5  7  Illumina  [5]
Total  2  1  23  29  2
Quality of care indicators  Odds ratio  95% Confidence interval  P-value
HbA1c test 2+ times during year?  1.01  (0.89, 1.14)  0.848
Feet checked during year?  0.91  (0.83, 1.00)  0.066
Dilated eye exam during year?  1.14  (1.04, 1.24)  0.003**
Blood pressure checked by doctor during year?  1.44  (1.13, 1.84)  0.004**
Visited doctor during last 12 months?  1.39  (1.22, 1.58)  <0.001***
Additionally, the table delineates the odds ratio and corresponding 95% confidence intervals, providing insight into the
statistical significance of these observations. The findings underscore the importance of considering comorbidities in the
assessment of treatment efficacy and patient outcomes, particularly in the context of viral infections like H1N1. Further
exploration of these metrics may yield critical insights for tailoring therapeutic strategies and improving overall patient care
within affected populations.
